papers.title_en: Observational and Dynamical Constraints on an Unseen Outer Perturber in the GJ 436 Hot Neptune
papers.authors: Haedam Im, Tiger Lu, Malena Rice, Quang H. Tran, Gongjie Li, Smadar Naoz
papers.evidence: 行星系统中的动力学过程与轨道架构
papers.tldr: 本研究探讨了热海王星GJ 436 b极地且高偏心率轨道的成因，通过分析20年的径向速度数据和Hipparcos-Gaia天体测量数据，对潜在的外部扰动天体进行了观测约束。结合三体层级长期动力学模拟，研究发现该系统的轨道架构可能由一个亚木星质量的伴星通过ZLK机制诱导形成。该工作为理解亚海王星荒漠行星的演化提供了重要约束，并缩小了未来伴星搜索的参数范围。
papers.abstract_en: Hot Neptunes in the sub-Jovian desert offer unique insights into planetary system evolution, retaining signatures of dynamical processes that shaped their present-day architectures. Many of these planets exhibit polar orbits, yet the mechanisms responsible for these misalignments between the stellar spin axis and planet orbit normal remain under debate. GJ 436 b stands among the very few hot Neptunes with both a polar and an eccentric orbit, thereby preserving dynamical signatures that may have otherwise been erased by tidal circularization. We investigate the unusual orbital architecture of GJ 436, exploring von Zeipel-Lidov-Kozai migration induced by a distant companion as a mechanism to explain the present-day orbit of GJ 436 b. Using ~20 years of archival radial velocity measurements and astrometric data from the Hipparcos-Gaia Catalog of Accelerations, we constrain a potential companion to ac < 5.4 AU for mc > 0.05 MJup and ac < 64 AU for mc > 24 MJup in the GJ 436 system at the 2 sigma confidence level, providing the most stringent constraints to date. We further perform three-body hierarchical secular simulations to determine which companion configurations could reproduce GJ 436 b's present-day orbit within the observationally constrained parameter space. Our dynamical modeling favors sub-Jovian masses on orbits with ac greater than roughly 6.8 AU, suggesting a substellar perturber. These observational and dynamical constraints can guide future companion searches and illuminate formation mechanisms for hot Neptune desert planets on polar orbits.
